atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports persistent discomfort.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports chest pain three months prior.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports chest pain resolved after four days.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies heartburn or indigestion prior to this event.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports difficulty swallowing approximately three months ago.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient attributes difficulty swallowing to a recent job transition.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies any family history of gastrointestinal problems.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient had her gall bladder removed several years ago.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical examination reveals mild tenderness to light palpation in the upper abdominal quadrants.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: No tenderness to deep palpation was noted.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's vital signs are within normal limits.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: No acute findings were noted on physical examination.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's presentation is concerning for potential esophageal or gastrointestinal etiology

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has a history of chest pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has a history of dysphagia

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Musculoskeletal etiologies should be considered

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Anxiety related to the recent job transition should be considered

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Cardiac etiology has been ruled out

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has elected to monitor for any additional symptoms

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient will call the office if she experiences any recurrence of chest pain or dysphagia

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: We will schedule an upper endoscopy if symptoms persist or worsen

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Follow up in four weeks to assess for symptom resolution
